name: User with Temporary Access Performing Suspicious Activities (AWS)
notes: This rule triggers when a user with temporary access performs suspicious activities. On AWS, assume role returns temporary credentials to resources the user has requested access for. This alone does not indicate an attack as the credentials may not even be used or the user may be legitimate but further activities should be monitored.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Amazon AWS CloudTrail
when any of Access Key ID (custom) are contained in any of Temporary Access Keys - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities
TD_list: APPLY User with Temporary Access Performing Suspicious Activities (AWS) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Amazon AWS CloudTrail
AND when any of Access Key ID (custom) are contained in any of Temporary Access Keys - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities
RED_list: Event name: User with Temporary Access Performing Suspicious Activities (AWS)
Event description: Detects a user with temporary access performing suspicious activities.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Profile (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
